  • QHOT.traps.QLetterArray

Source code for QHOT.traps.QLetterArray

import numpy as np
from QHOT.traps.QTrapArray import QTrapArray


# 7-row x 5-column dot-matrix font.
# Each character is a list of 7 integers (one per row, top to bottom).
# Each integer is a 5-bit mask: bit 4 = leftmost column, bit 0 = rightmost.
_FONT = {
    'A': [0x0E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x1F, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11],
    'B': [0x1E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x1E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x1E],
    'C': [0x0E, 0x11, 0x10, 0x10, 0x10, 0x11, 0x0E],
    'D': [0x1E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x1E],
    'E': [0x1F, 0x10, 0x10, 0x1E, 0x10, 0x10, 0x1F],
    'F': [0x1F, 0x10, 0x10, 0x1E, 0x10, 0x10, 0x10],
    'G': [0x0E, 0x11, 0x10, 0x17, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0F],
    'H': [0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x1F, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11],
    'I': [0x0E, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x0E],
    'J': [0x07, 0x02, 0x02, 0x02, 0x02, 0x12, 0x0C],
    'K': [0x11, 0x12, 0x14, 0x18, 0x14, 0x12, 0x11],
    'L': [0x10, 0x10, 0x10, 0x10, 0x10, 0x10, 0x1F],
    'M': [0x11, 0x1B, 0x15, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11],
    'N': [0x11, 0x19, 0x15, 0x13, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11],
    'O': [0x0E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0E],
    'P': [0x1E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x1E, 0x10, 0x10, 0x10],
    'Q': [0x0E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x15, 0x12, 0x0D],
    'R': [0x1E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x1E, 0x14, 0x12, 0x11],
    'S': [0x0F, 0x10, 0x10, 0x0E, 0x01, 0x01, 0x1E],
    'T': [0x1F, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04],
    'U': [0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0E],
    'V': [0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0A, 0x04],
    'W': [0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x15, 0x15, 0x1B, 0x11],
    'X': [0x11, 0x11, 0x0A, 0x04, 0x0A, 0x11, 0x11],
    'Y': [0x11, 0x11, 0x0A, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04],
    'Z': [0x1F, 0x01, 0x02, 0x04, 0x08, 0x10, 0x1F],
    '0': [0x0E, 0x11, 0x13, 0x15, 0x19, 0x11, 0x0E],
    '1': [0x04, 0x0C, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x0E],
    '2': [0x0E, 0x11, 0x01, 0x06, 0x08, 0x10, 0x1F],
    '3': [0x1F, 0x01, 0x02, 0x06, 0x01, 0x11, 0x0E],
    '4': [0x02, 0x06, 0x0A, 0x12, 0x1F, 0x02, 0x02],
    '5': [0x1F, 0x10, 0x1E, 0x01, 0x01, 0x11, 0x0E],
    '6': [0x06, 0x08, 0x10, 0x1E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0E],
    '7': [0x1F, 0x01, 0x02, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04],
    '8': [0x0E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0E],
    '9': [0x0E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0F, 0x01, 0x02, 0x0C],
    ' ': [0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00],
    # Lowercase letters (rows 0-1 = ascender zone, rows 5-6 = descender zone)
    'a': [0x00, 0x00, 0x0E, 0x01, 0x0F, 0x11, 0x0F],
    'b': [0x10, 0x10, 0x1E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x1E],
    'c': [0x00, 0x00, 0x0E, 0x10, 0x10, 0x11, 0x0E],
    'd': [0x01, 0x01, 0x0F, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0F],
    'e': [0x00, 0x00, 0x0E, 0x11, 0x1F, 0x10, 0x0E],
    'f': [0x06, 0x08, 0x1E, 0x08, 0x08, 0x08, 0x08],
    'g': [0x00, 0x0F, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0F, 0x01, 0x0E],
    'h': [0x10, 0x10, 0x16, 0x19, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11],
    'i': [0x04, 0x00, 0x0C, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x0E],
    'j': [0x02, 0x00, 0x06, 0x02, 0x02, 0x12, 0x0C],
    'k': [0x10, 0x10, 0x12, 0x14, 0x18, 0x14, 0x12],
    'l': [0x0C, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x0E],
    'm': [0x00, 0x00, 0x1A, 0x15, 0x15, 0x11, 0x11],
    'n': [0x00, 0x00, 0x16, 0x19, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11],
    'o': [0x00, 0x00, 0x0E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0E],
    'p': [0x00, 0x1E, 0x11, 0x11, 0x1E, 0x10, 0x10],
    'q': [0x00, 0x0F, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0F, 0x01, 0x01],
    'r': [0x00, 0x00, 0x16, 0x18, 0x10, 0x10, 0x10],
    's': [0x00, 0x00, 0x0F, 0x10, 0x0E, 0x01, 0x1E],
    't': [0x04, 0x04, 0x1E, 0x04, 0x04, 0x04, 0x03],
    'u': [0x00, 0x00, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x13, 0x0D],
    'v': [0x00, 0x00, 0x11, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0A, 0x04],
    'w': [0x00, 0x00, 0x11, 0x11, 0x15, 0x1B, 0x11],
    'x': [0x00, 0x00, 0x11, 0x0A, 0x04, 0x0A, 0x11],
    'y': [0x00, 0x00, 0x11, 0x11, 0x0F, 0x01, 0x0E],
    'z': [0x00, 0x00, 0x1F, 0x02, 0x04, 0x08, 0x1F],
}


def _char_positions(char: str) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
    '''Return (col, row) pairs for lit dots in a 5x7 character cell.

    Looks up the character directly (supporting distinct upper- and
    lower-case glyphs), falls back to the uppercase glyph, then to
    space for unrecognised characters.

    The origin is the top-left of the cell.  Column increases to the
    right; row increases downward.
    '''
    rows = _FONT.get(char, _FONT.get(char.upper(), _FONT[' ']))
    positions = []
    for row_idx, bits in enumerate(rows):
        for col_idx in range(5):
            if bits & (1 << (4 - col_idx)):
                positions.append((col_idx, row_idx))
    return positions


def _char_mask(char: str) -> np.ndarray:
    '''Return a (5, 7) boolean mask for the given character.

    Index [col, row] is True when the dot at that position is lit.
    '''
    mask = np.zeros((5, 7), dtype=bool)
    for col, row in _char_positions(char):
        mask[col, row] = True
    return mask




[docs]
class QLetterArray(QTrapArray):

    '''Single dot-matrix character rendered as optical tweezers.

    A specialisation of ``QTrapArray`` with a fixed 5-column × 7-row
    grid and a mask derived from the dot-matrix font.  Each lit dot
    becomes one ``QTweezer``; blank dots produce no trap.

    Characters A-Z, a-z, 0-9 and space are supported.  Upper- and
    lower-case letters use distinct glyphs.  Unrecognised characters
    are rendered as spaces.

    The ``nx`` and ``ny`` properties are fixed at 5 and 7 and are not
    exposed to ``QTrapWidget``.  Only ``separation`` is registered.

    Inherits
    --------
    QFab.traps.QTrapArray

    Parameters
    ----------
    char : str
        Single character to render.  Default: ``'A'``.
    separation : float
        Center-to-center dot spacing [pixels].  Default: ``40.``.
    *args, **kwargs
        Forwarded to ``QTrapArray``.

    Attributes
    ----------
    char : str
        Current character.  Setting this updates the mask and repopulates.
    separation : float
        Dot spacing [pixels].  Registered property.
    '''

    def __init__(self, *args,
                 char: str = 'A',
                 separation: float = 40.,
                 **kwargs) -> None:
        self._char = str(char)
        super().__init__(*args,
                         shape=(5, 7),
                         separation=separation,
                         mask=_char_mask(char),
                         **kwargs)

    def _registerProperties(self) -> None:
        super()._registerProperties()
        # nx, ny, and fuzz are not meaningful for a fixed-shape letter cell.
        self.properties.pop('nx', None)
        self.properties.pop('ny', None)
        self.properties.pop('fuzz', None)

    @property
    def char(self) -> str:
        '''Single character rendered by this array.'''
        return self._char

    @char.setter
    def char(self, char: str) -> None:
        self._char = str(char)
        self.mask = _char_mask(char)  # triggers _repopulate via mask.setter



[docs]
    @classmethod
    def example(cls) -> None:  # pragma: no cover
        '''Demonstrate creation and character change.'''
        la = cls(char='N', separation=10.)
        print(la, f'  ({len(list(la.leaves()))} dots)')
        la.char = 'Y'
        print(la, f'  ({len(list(la.leaves()))} dots)')






if __name__ == '__main__':  # pragma: no cover
    QLetterArray.example()
